label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we can infer from the passage about volleyball. The options are A to D. Let me go through the passage again to pick out the relevant parts. First, the passage starts by saying volleyball was created in 1895 by William G. Morgan, influenced by basketball. He wanted a game suitable for middle-aged men. He made a game called "mintonette" which is similar to badminton, using hands instead of equipment to cut costs. The game became popular and spread. Then it mentions that several changes were made to the game, and specific rules were set. The rules included court size, number of players, hits per team, rotation, and standard size of the ball. The rules continued to change until the game became what we know today. Looking at the options: A) Volleyball develops from the game of "mintonette". The passage says Morgan created "mintonette", which became volleyball. So yes, A seems correct. The original game was called mintonette, and that evolved into volleyball. B) Most of the competitive volleyball teams are in college. The passage mentions high school, college, women, men, children, etc., but doesn't say that college is the most. So B is not supported. C) The rules continue to change as they are easy to learn. The passage says the rules changed until the game became what we have today, but the reason given is not because they are easy to learn. The ease of learning is mentioned as a reason for the game's popularity, not the rules changing. So C is incorrect. D) Different rules have appeared to cut down the costs. The passage
